author: Ahmed Ossama | Product Leader, Builder & Venture Management Architect
scope_rule: Only automations declared in this registry may be triggered by the skill. No automation may be improvised or added during a session. Scope is locked at this file.

automations.name: Job Board Fetch and Search
automations.description: Fetch job listings from job boards using web_search and web_fetch. Parse results into the ranked discovery table.
automations.fallback: User runs searches manually and pastes results
automations.scope_boundary: Job search queries only. No login to job platforms without user credential consent. No bulk scraping.

platform_notes.linkedin: Search results via web_search only. Direct fetch often blocked.
platform_notes.indeed: Search results via web_search only. Direct fetch often blocked.
platform_notes.greenhouse: Direct fetch reliable. Full JD accessible.
platform_notes.lever: Direct fetch reliable. Full JD accessible.
platform_notes.breezy_hr: Direct fetch reliable. Full JD accessible.
platform_notes.wellfound: Direct fetch reliable for public listings.

automations.name: Full Job Posting Fetch for Top 5
automations.description: After the ranked discovery table is presented and scored, fetch the full JD content for the top 5 flagged results to give the user complete information before selecting a role.
automations.scope_boundary: Top 5 flagged results only. No bulk fetch beyond that without user request.

automations.name: Application Form Fetch and Parse
automations.description: Fetch the application form for the selected role. Parse all visible fields and custom questions. Flag template-variable sections for manual paste.
automations.fallback: User opens form in browser and pastes custom questions manually

automations.name: Browser Form Navigation and Field Filling
automations.phase: Phase 6 — after Governance Gate passes at score 5
automations.description: Use browser automation to navigate to the application form, map each confirmed answer from the application package to the correct form field, and fill all fields. Does NOT submit. Submission is a separate action (A05).
automations.fallback: Pre-staged numbered answer list for manual field-by-field paste
automations.prerequisite: All 8 Governance Gate checks must pass before this action is offered
automations.failure_action: Stop immediately. Report which field failed. Show pre-staged answers for remaining fields. Switch to manual. Never auto-retry.
automations.high_bot_detection_note: LinkedIn and Indeed block browser automation reliably. Fallback to manual for these platforms.
automations.scope_boundary: Fields from the confirmed application package only. No field may be filled with data not in the package.

automations.name: Application Submission
automations.phase: Phase 6 — after A04 field fill is reviewed by user
automations.description: Submit the filled application form via browser automation. This action is irreversible. Requires the highest consent gate.
automations.fallback: User clicks Submit manually after field filling
automations.prerequisite: A04 must complete and user must review the filled form before A05 is offered
consent_gate_content: Confirmation that this cannot be undone
automations.scope_boundary: Only the specific application being processed in this session. No bulk submission. One application per consent gate.

automations.name: Cover Letter DOCX Creation
automations.description: Generate a formatted DOCX file containing the humanized cover letter. Font matches the standard professional format. Presented for local download immediately.
automations.fallback: Cover letter text provided inline for manual copy-paste into a document
automations.output_format: DOCX — professional format, matching CV font conventions
automations.note: Local download is always offered first. Cloud save is a separate action (A09).

automations.name: Full Application Package Document Creation
automations.phase: Phase 6 — after all 8 Governance Gate checks pass
automations.description: Generate a complete session record document containing the full application package: all form field answers, cover letter, custom question responses, company intelligence summary, fit analysis verdict, and salary figure used. Presented for local download.
automations.fallback: All outputs already visible in conversation for manual save
automations.output_format: DOCX or PDF — candidate's choice at time of creation
automations.note: Cloud save is a separate action (A09).

automations.name: Hiring Manager or Recruiter Email Draft and Send
automations.phase: Phase 3 — Clarifying Intake (if follow-up needed) or Phase 7 — Post-Submission
automations.description: Draft and send an email to the hiring manager or recruiter. Phase 3 use case: requesting missing information (e.g. application form name, role clarification). Phase 7 use case: post-submission follow-up after agreed interval.
mcp_required: Gmail MCP or Outlook/MS365 MCP — user selects per session
automations.fallback: Email draft presented for manual sending by user
automations.recipient_scope: Hiring manager or recruiter at the target company only. No other recipients.
consent_gate_content: Confirmation that this cannot be recalled
automations.scope_boundary: Professional communications directly related to the active application only. No marketing emails. No mass sends. No emails to people outside the target company's hiring team.

automations.name: Cloud Document Save
automations.phase: After any document creation action (A06 or A07)
automations.description: Save a created document to the user's cloud storage. Always offered AFTER local download is presented. User selects which provider. Folder path is suggested and confirmed before save.
mcp_required: Google Drive MCP or OneDrive/MS365 MCP — user selects
automations.fallback: File already downloaded locally — cloud save simply skipped
automations.sequence_rule: Local download via present_files MUST be offered first. Cloud save MUST NOT be offered before local download.
automations.folder_confirmation: Suggest path, wait for user to confirm or modify before saving

automations.name: Calendar Follow-Up Reminder
automations.description: Create a calendar event to remind the user to follow up on the submitted application after an agreed number of days (typically 5–7 business days).
automations.fallback: Follow-up date noted in conversation for manual calendar entry

event_content.title: Follow up: [ROLE_TITLE] at [COMPANY_NAME]
event_content.description: Application submitted on [SUBMISSION_DATE]. Follow up by email or phone.

automations.scope_boundary: One reminder per application. Job search context only.

automations.name: Confirmation Email to Self
automations.description: Send the full application package to the user's own email address as a personal record of what was submitted.
mcp_required: Gmail MCP or Outlook/MS365 MCP — user selects
automations.fallback: Application package already in conversation — user saves manually
automations.recipient_scope: User's own email address only. Never sent to a third party.

automations.name: Job Board MCP — Authenticated Job Search
automations.phase: Phase 0 — Job Discovery (when job board MCP is ACTIVE)
automations.description: Use an active job board or ATS platform MCP to perform authenticated searches, access saved jobs, retrieve application status, or trigger Easy Apply flows. Supplements web_search and web_fetch with authenticated access to platform features not available to anonymous requests.
mcp_required: Breezy HR MCP — whichever is ACTIVE in session
automations.fallback: web_search and web_fetch for discovery — fully functional without MCP
automations.scope_boundary: Read-only job discovery and search only. No application submission via this automation — submission is A05. No bulk scraping. No storing of credentials beyond current session.
automations.note: When multiple job board MCPs are active, the skill uses the most appropriate one for the target platform and informs the user which is being used.

automations.name: Job Board MCP Discovery Search
automations.phase: A0 — Capability Detection (STEP 1C — runs when all job board MCPs are INACTIVE)
automations.description: When no job board or ATS platform MCP is detected in the session, run web searches to find currently available MCPs for job platforms. Present results as RECOMMENDED CONNECTIONS in the Capability Map. This automation helps the user discover and connect MCPs that would enhance future sessions.
automations.fallback: Skip — if web_search is also unavailable, note that MCP recommendations cannot be retrieved and proceed
search_queries: LinkedIn MCP model context protocol job search site:github.com
job board ATS MCP Claude integration available site:github.com OR site:npmjs.com
Greenhouse Lever Workday MCP server integration Claude
automations.scope_boundary: Search and recommendation only. Does not install, connect, or configure any MCP. User must connect MCPs themselves via their platform settings.

artifact_policy.present_files_inactive_fallback: When present_files is INACTIVE in A0, follow SKILL.md section A0 — Mandatory artifact fallback: deliver full artifact content inline (monospace or structured text) and instruct the user to save manually. Never assume a host file-download primitive exists.

out_of_scope_examples: Accessing candidate accounts on job platforms without session credentials
Sending emails to anyone other than hiring manager, recruiter, or user's own address
Creating documents outside the application package scope
Any automation that modifies existing files in external systems without preview
Any action on platforms not listed in this registry
Bulk application submission across multiple companies
Automated follow-up emails without explicit per-email consent
Any action that executes based on a score of 5 — scoring and approval are separate
Installing or auto-connecting MCPs without user action
Using a job board MCP for submission — submission always uses A05 (BrowserBase/Playwright)
